All prices as of 2045 GMT

EQUITIES

GLOBAL - Oil futures sank below $100 a barrel while equity and bond prices rallied sharply on Wednesday after the United States and Iran agreed on a two-week ceasefire, prompting hopes for a resumption of oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI rose 29.33 points, or 2.94%, to 1,027.42

For a full report, click on

- - - -

NEW YORK - U.S. stocks closed sharply higher on Wednesday after a last-minute, two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran lifted investor sentiment.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 1,326.33 points, or 2.85%, to 47,910.79, the S&P 500 gained 165.98 points, or 2.51%, to 6,782.83 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 617.15 points, or 2.80%, to 22,635.00.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - European shares jumped more than 3% on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, sparking a relief rally across global markets with investors optimistictrade through the Strait of Hormuz could resume soon.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index was up 3.7% at 612.32 points, posting its biggest one-day gain in a year.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Japan's blue-chip share average jumped by the most in a year, while the country's bonds and currency rallied on Wednesday, as the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement drove crude oil prices sharply lower and eased concerns of an economic slowdown.

The Nikkei 225 Index rose 5.39% to 56,308.42, climbing for a fourth straight day to hit its highest close since March 2.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - China and Hong Kong stocks jumped on Wednesday as a U.S. ceasefire deal with Iran lifted investors' appetite for risk.

China's blue-chip CSI300 Index jumped 3.5%, its biggest daily gain since October 2024.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - Australian shares are expected to open little changed on Thursday, with miners and energy stocks expected to be pressured by weaker commodity prices, likely offsetting broader relief‑rally gains, even as doubts over the durability of the tentative U.S.-Iran ceasefire persist.

The local share price index futures (YAPcm1) fell 0.2%, a 21.2-point premium to the underlying S&P/ASX 200 index close. The benchmark rose 2.6% on Wednesday.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - South Korean shares rose nearly 7% on Wednesday to close at the highest level since mid-March as the United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, sparking a relief rally.

The benchmark KOSPI closed up 377.56 points, or 6.87%, at 5,872.34.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

FOREIGN EXCHANGE

NEW YORK - The dollar sank to its lowest level in a month against a basket of major currencies on Wednesday after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire that injected optimism into risk assets worldwide.

The dollar index, which measures the greenback's performance against a basket of six currencies, was last up 0.16% at 99.077. The dollar is still above where it was prior to the onset of the war, showing investor sentiment has not fully recovered.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

CHINA - China's yuan jumped to a more than three-year high against the U.S. dollar as the greenback dropped after U.S. President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran.

The onshore yuan hit 6.8287 per dollar in morning trade on Wednesday.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - The Australian and New Zealand dollars were swept higher on Wednesday as a tentative ceasefire in the Gulf triggered a relief rally across risk assets, while the diminished risk of runaway inflation boosted bonds.

The Aussie climbed 1.1% to $0.7053 , a marked turnaround from its recent trough of $0.6834. Resistance now lies at $0.7124 and $0.71875.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - The Korean won strengthened against dollar on Wednesday.

The won was quoted at 1,470.6 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 2.07% higher than its previous close at 1,501.0.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TREASURIES

NEW YORK - U.S. Treasury yields tumbled on Wednesday after a two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran ignited a relief rally across asset classes as plummeting oil prices revived the possibility of interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve this year.

The yield on the benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury note (US10YT=TWEB) fell 7.9 basis points to 4.264%

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - Traders scaled back bets on future European Central Bank rate hikes after a deal for a two‑week ceasefire in the Iran conflict but still priced in two tightening moves by the end of the year amid uncertainty over whether a lasting settlement will be reached.

Germany’s 10-year government bond yield (DE10YT=RR) fell 14 basis points (bps) to 2.945%. It had hit 3.13% in late March, its highest level since June 2011, compared to 2.65% at the end of February, just before the outbreak of the war.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Benchmark Japanese government bonds (JGBs) rallied on Wednesday as early signs of a pause in hostilities in the Middle East eased worries about rising energy costs and inflation.

The 10-year JGB yield (JP10YTN=JBTC) fell 5 basis points (bps) to 2.355%.

For a full report, click on

COMMODITIES

GOLD

Gold scaled nearly a three-week peak on Wednesday, as the U.S. dollar and oil prices fell after Washington and Tehran agreed to a two-week truce in their war, calming inflation worries.

Spot gold was up 1.2% at $4,756.37 per ounce, as of 11:43 a.m. EDT (1543 GMT).

For a full report, click on

- - - -

IRON ORE

Iron ore futures prices fell on Wednesday, as shipments of the key steelmaking ingredient from major suppliers surged and U.S. President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran.

The most-traded iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) closed daytime trade 1.44% lower at 789 yuan ($115.60) a metric ton.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

BASE METALS

Copper jumped to a three-week high on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, easing fears of a global economic slowdown as a result of the Middle East conflict.

Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange was up 3.2% at $12,699.50 a metric ton as of 1600 GMT, having earlier risen as much as 3.6% to $12,755.50, its highest since March 18. Copper fell by 7.6% in March on economic fears sparked by the Iran war.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

OIL

Oil plummeted on Wednesday, falling below $100 a barrel, on hope of the Strait of Hormuz reopening after U.S. President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran.

Brent crude futures settled $14.52, or 13.29%, lower at $94.75 a barrel.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

PALM OIL

Malaysian palm oil futures slid more than 3% on Wednesday, hitting their lowest in two weeks, as a sharp drop in crude oil prices following a U.S-Iran ceasefire deal weighed on the market.

The benchmark palm oil contract for June delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange closed179 ringgit lower, or down 3.76%, at 4,586 ringgit ($1,154.00) a metric ton, the lowest since March 26.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

RUBBER

Japanese rubber futures fell for the first time in five sessions on Wednesday, as oil plummeted after a ceasefire agreement was reached in the Middle East conflict, with Iran allowing the safe passage of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz.

The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for September delivery JRUc6, 0#2JRU: was down 0.9 yen, or 0.23%, at 396.5 yen ($2.51) per kg.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

data.title: Everspin Signs Deal With Microchip Technology to Boost Manufacturing
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Everspin Technologies signed an agreement with Microchip Technology to expand its onshore production capacity of Magnetoresistive Random Access Memory and Tunnel Magnetoresistive sensor products.

Under the agreement, Everspin will establish a manufacturing line at a Microchip fabrication facility located in Oregon. The agreement has an initial term of 10 years and can be extended in two-year increments, Everspin said Wednesday.

Everspin will own the intellectual property and manufacturing process, while leveraging Microchip's foundry services capacity, the company said. Financial terms of the agreement weren't disclosed.

The first products from the collaboration are expected to ship in the second half of 2027.

The pact will increase wafer capacity to support growth, boost supply into the 2030s, create additional research and development opportunities, Everspin said, and provide a second onshore source for MRAM and TMR products.

"Everspin is expanding its manufacturing footprint to support the next phase of MRAM adoption, as customers look for both long-term supply stability and higher-density, more capable solutions," Chief Executive Sanjeev Aggarwal said. "Our partnership with Microchip adds the production scale to support demand while continuing to advance our MRAM roadmap."

Everspin will continue manufacturing products at its facility in Chandler, Ariz.

Shares of Everspin rose 5.6% to $10.30 in after-hours trading Wednesday.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: RBC Leads $1.1 Billion Financing for EnergySolutions Buyout Amid Volatile Markets
data.description:
Royal Bank of Canada is stepping in to anchor a $1.1 billion loan package backing Energy Capital Partners' renewed push to acquire EnergySolutions, a setup that could offer an early read on investor appetite for large leveraged financings. The bank is weighing a syndication to institutional investors as soon as June, though timing could shift, reflecting how unsettled credit markets remain. The structure and reception of this deal may matter more than usual, particularly as lenders navigate a backdrop that has recently tested distribution capacity.

Energy Capital Partners is re-acquiring EnergySolutions just four years after selling a majority stake, leaning back into a business tied to nuclear services at a moment when power demand narratives are shifting. Interest in nuclear-linked assets has been building alongside the rise of artificial intelligence infrastructure and broader electrification trends, both of which are driving incremental electricity demand. That backdrop could be supporting renewed sponsor conviction, even as financing conditions remain uneven.

Execution, however, could be the key variable. Banks have been contending with investor pushback on other large buyout financings, and recent deals have required multiple rounds of concessions to clear the market. Activity levels have also slowed, with March deal volumes dropping sharply year over year and only a limited number of US leveraged loans currently in the pipeline, suggesting this transaction could serve as a near-term signal for how much risk investors are willing to absorb.

data.title: Universal Logistics Appoints Rogers as CFO, Succeeding Beres
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Universal Logistics Holdings appointed Michael Rogers as its next finance chief and treasurer, succeeding Jude Beres.

Beres will resign, effective May 29, to pursue opportunities outside of the transportation and logistics industry, the company said Wednesday.

Rogers currently serves as chief financial officer of Conlan Tire, Hercules Materials Holdings, and some of the two companies' privately held affiliates. Before that, he held several finance leadership roles at Ford.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: CBOT corn ends lower, following as crude tumbles on US-Iran ceasefire
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade corn futures fell on Wednesday, as crude oil futures plunged below $100 a barrel after U.S. President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, traders said.

  • Corn sometimes follows energy prices due to its role as a feedstock for ethanol fuel.

  • Also, managed commodity funds hold a sizable net long position in CBOT corn futures, leaving the market vulnerable to bouts of long liquidation.

  • CBOT May corn (CK26) settled down 1-3/4 cents at $4.47-1/4 per bushel, paring losses after a dip to $4.42-1/4, the contract's lowest since March 4.

  • CBOT new-crop December corn (CZ26) ended down 2 cents at $4.76 a bushel.

  • South Korean feedmakers were active in booking corn on the global marketplace after CBOT corn futures opened sharply lower.

  • The U.S. Energy Information Administration said weekly production of corn-based ethanol rose in the week ended April 3 to 1.116 million barrels per day, a two-week high. Weekly U.S. ethanol stockpiles rose to 26.053 million barrels, up 62,000 from the prior week.

  • Ahead of the U.S. Department of Agriculture's monthly supply/demand report on Thursday, analysts surveyed by Reuters on average expected only modest changes in the government's forecasts for U.S. and global 2025/26 corn ending stocks.

  • Ahead of Thursday's weekly USDA export sales report, traders expected the government to report sales of U.S. old-crop corn in the week ended April 2 at 750,000 to 1,600,000 tons.

data.title: Alibaba Stock Jumps. Builds AI Data Center Without Nvidia Chips
data.description:
Alibaba rose about 4% on Wednesday after the e-commerce giant said it and China Telecom are building a data center in southern China using the company's own semiconductors.

The facility will feature 10,000 of Alibaba's Zhenwu chips, designed for AI training and inferencing. The cluster can support AI models with hundreds of billions of parameters, the South China Morning Post reported.

The move appears to be part of China's push to develop domestic AI infrastructure as competition with U.S. rivals intensifies. Alibaba has been designing its own chips through its T-head unit and is also a major cloud computing provider in China.

The development comes as the U.S. has restricted China's access to key semiconductor technology, including AI chips from Nvidia (NVDA). Those restrictions may have accelerated China's efforts to develop home-grown alternatives, according to the report.

data.title: Avalyn Pharma Files for IPO
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Avalyn Pharma has filed for an initial public offering.

The biopharmaceutical company applied to list its stock on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker AVLN, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday.

The company did not disclose the expected price or size of the offering.

The company has also granted underwriters an option to purchase an additional, undisclosed number of units to cover over-allotments. Morgan Stanley, Jefferies, Evercore ISI and Guggenheim Securities are listed as the offering's underwriter.

Avalyn is working to develop inhaled treatments for rare respiratory diseases. Its current pipeline of potential treatments is focused on pulmonary fibrosis.

The company plans to use the proceeds from the offering to fund the development of its two clinical-stage programs and preclinical-stage program, as well as to fund research and development activities for additional programs.

Avalyn recorded a loss of $85.2 million, or $4.56 a share, in 2025, compared to a loss of $49.7 million, or $9.57 a share, a year earlier.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Franklin Covey Co Q2 FY2026: Revenue $59.65M, EPS $(0.17) — 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Franklin Covey Co reported essentially flat revenue in Q2 FY2026 while swinging deeper into a net loss as subscription momentum and education growth were offset by softness in Enterprise North America and restructuring impacts.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $59.65M for Q2 FY2026, essentially flat vs. $59.61M in the year-ago quarter (+0.06% YoY).
  • Net income: $(1.982)M for Q2 FY2026, vs. $(1.076)M in the year-ago quarter (worsened YoY).
  • Diluted EPS: $(0.17) for Q2 FY2026, vs. $(0.08) in the year-ago quarter (decline YoY).

Business Highlights

  • Consolidated quarterly revenue was flat at $59.6M. The Education segment grew 16% year-over-year, driven by Leader in Me adoption and increased training and coaching delivery.
  • Leader in Me added +624 new schools in the quarter and training/coaching delivery rose by roughly 300 additional days, supporting subscription and services demand.
  • Enterprise North America declined, reflecting prior-period invoiced shortfalls that weighed on segment performance.
  • Subscription and subscription services invoiced amounts rose strongly (a 16% quarter-over-quarter lead metric), indicating go-to-market momentum.
  • Management completed a restructuring to reduce costs and implemented a new North America go-to-market and sales structure aimed at improving execution.

Original SEC Filing: FRANKLIN COVEY CO [ FC ] - 10-Q - Apr. 08, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Colombia buys back $1.47 bln of domestic debt, finance ministry says
data.description:
Colombia has repurchased 5.4 trillion pesos ($1.47 billion) of domestic government bonds, its finance ministry said on Wednesday.

The bonds, known as TES, are the government's second-largest source of funds for public spending, after tax revenues.

The finance ministry said it received bids totaling 6.5 trillion pesos ($1.77 billion) for the bonds, maturing in 2036, 2040, 2042, 2046, 2050, and 2058.

The buyback likely generated interest savings of 2.1 trillion pesos ($570.9 million), it added, saying the trasaction would improve Colombia's domestic public debt profile.

(1 dollar = 3,678.19 pesos)

data.title: Caterpillar Names Incoming Chief Financial Officer
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Caterpillar appointed Kyle Epley as chief financial officer, effective May 1.

Epley, a veteran of the construction equipment company, will succeed Andrew Bonfield, who will transition to an advisory role before retiring Oct. 1, Caterpillar said Wednesday.

Epley brings nearly three decades of experience to the role, the company said. He is currently senior vice president of global finance services and previously worked as division CFO and corporate controller.

Bonfield has been CFO of Caterpillar since 2018.

Write to Chris Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.description:

data.title: XMax Engages Cloud Alliance for AWS AI Inference Platform in $400,000 Services Deal
data.description:
XMax entered into an AI Inference Platform Deployment and Service Agreement with Cloud Alliance, effective April 1, 2026. The $400,000 fixed-fee arrangement covers development, deployment, and necessary configuration of an AI inference platform in the company’s designated AWS environment. XMax will make a $200,000 non-refundable mobilization payment shortly after signing, with the remaining $200,000 due upon written acceptance. The project is expected to accelerate deployment of production-ready AI capabilities for an approved use case.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: AI Inference Platform Deployment and Service Agreement
  • Counterparty: Cloud Alliance
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 06 2026 / Apr 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Deploy AI inference platform on AWS for approved use case

Original SEC Filing: XMax Inc. [ XWIN ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

data.title: Richardson Electronics reports Q3 net income $0.9M, revenue $55.5M; Board declares $0.06 dividend
data.description:
Richardson Electronics reported third-quarter fiscal 2026 net income of $0.9 million (diluted EPS $0.07) on net sales of $55.5 million, a 3.1% year‑over‑year increase. The company posted gross profit of $17.7 million (31.9% of net sales) and operating income of $1.5 million for the quarter. The board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.06 per common share payable May 27, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Net sales for Q3 FY2026 were $55.5 million, up 3.1% versus Q3 FY2025 ($53.8 million).
  • Gross profit and margin: Gross profit for Q3 FY2026 was $17.68 million, representing 31.9% of net sales.
  • Operating income: Operating income for the quarter was $1.5 million.
  • Net income and EPS: Net income was $0.9 million; diluted earnings per common share were $0.07 for the quarter.
  • Adjusted / Non‑GAAP metrics: EBITDA for Q3 FY2026 was $2.2 million; non‑GAAP net income per diluted share was $0.07 (adjusted to exclude a prior one‑time Healthcare asset sale loss).

Business Highlights

  • Segment momentum: PMT led sales growth with Q3 PMT net sales of $38.7 million, a 9.7% increase year‑over‑year driven by semiconductor wafer fab and RF/microwave products.
  • Backlog growth: Total backlog increased 11.4 sequentially to $151.2 million at February 28, 2026, reaching the highest level in nearly three years and driven primarily by PMT orders.
  • Segment mix shifts: Since the January 2025 Healthcare asset sale, Healthcare was consolidated into PMT; excluding Healthcare, PMT net sales rose 14.5% in the quarter.
  • Operational investments: Company increased headcount and technical hires, incurring higher salaries, incentives and related benefits to support growth and product development efforts.
  • Balance sheet and liquidity: Cash and cash equivalents were $29.5 million at quarter end and the company had no outstanding borrowing on its revolving credit facility with PNC Bank.

Original SEC Filing: RICHARDSON ELECTRONICS, LTD. [ RELL ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Pioneer Power Sol posts $27.6M revenue in 2025; Q4 revenue $5.6M, net loss $6.0M
data.description:
Pioneer Power Sol reported full-year 2025 revenue of $27.6 million, up 20.8% year-over-year and in line with company guidance, while reporting a net loss of $6.0 million for the year. Fourth-quarter 2025 revenue was $5.6 million with a net loss of $1.4 million and gross margin of 23.5% for the quarter. The company disclosed $15.0 million in cash at year-end and a backlog of $12.6 million as of December 31, 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $27.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2025; $5.6 million for Q4 2025.
  • Gross profit: $3.4 million for full-year 2025 (12.4% gross margin); Q4 gross profit $1.3 million (23.5% gross margin).
  • Operating loss from continuing operations: $(6.6) million for full-year 2025; $(1.1) million for Q4 2025.
  • Net (loss) income: $(6.0) million for full-year 2025 (includes $449,000 from discontinued operations); Q4 net loss $(1.4) million (includes $(17,500) from discontinued operations).
  • Non-GAAP operating (loss) income from continuing operations: $(98,000) for 2025; $589,000 for Q4 2025 (non-GAAP excludes corporate overhead, R&D, D&A and non-recurring professional fees).

Business Highlights

  • Expanded product portfolio beyond mobile EV charging with the launch of the PRYMUS Mobile Distributed Energy Platform (designed for 1 MW–10 MW off-grid deployments) and the PowerCore Residential Prime Energy Platform targeting whole-home 24/7/365 resiliency with integrated high-speed charging.
  • Secured a strategic international agreement to scale the e-Boost mobile charging ecosystem globally using a franchise model and local partnerships to pursue capital-light expansion into international EV markets.
  • Maintained steady demand for e-Boost across core markets with an established leasing and service model generating recurring revenue and supporting the company’s distributed energy ecosystem.
  • Reported initial customer engagements for PRYMUS in Q1 2026 with shipments scheduled for 2027 and expected PowerCore shipments beginning in the second half of 2026, indicating progression from product launches toward commercial deployment.

Original SEC Filing: PIONEER POWER SOLUTIONS, INC. [ PPSI ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: FDA Lifts Partial Clinical Hold on MacroGenics' LINNET Study; Enrollment to Resume
data.description:
MacroGenics announced the FDA has removed the partial clinical hold on its Phase 2 LINNET study of lorigerlimab, allowing enrollment to resume.

Key Highlights:

  • FDA removed the partial clinical hold on the Phase 2 LINNET study of lorigerlimab, a PD-1/CTLA-4 bispecific DART molecule.
  • Previously enrolled participants may continue treatment; new participants will be enrolled under a revised protocol.
  • Revised protocol includes additional risk-mitigation measures for potential hematologic and cardiac toxicities.
  • LINNET evaluates single-agent lorigerlimab in up to ~60 patients with platinum-resistant ovarian or clear cell gynecologic cancers; 41 participants dosed to date.
  • Company remains on track to provide a mid-2026 clinical update on the program.

Original SEC Filing: MACROGENICS INC [ MGNX ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

data.title: Dynatrace to Buy Telemetry Provider Bindplane
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Dynatrace agreed to acquire Bindplane, a telemetry-pipeline provider.

Telemetry, which shows what is happening inside digital systems, is in higher demand because of the rise of artificial intelligence, Dynatrace said Wednesday.

Bindplane's technology improves telemetry-data quality, reduces ingest costs and enhances compliance by removing or encrypting sensitive data.

The deal is expected to close later this month and isn't expected to have a material effect on Dynatrace's fiscal 2027 financial results.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Costco Reports 11% Growth in March Sales
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Costco reported $28.41 billion in net sales for the retail month of March, up 11% from a year earlier.

The wholesale retailer reported a 9.4% increase in comparable sales during the five weeks ended April 5, including an 8.7% increase in the U.S., an 10.7% growth in Canada, and 11.9% growth in other international markets.

Digitally enabled comparable sales rose 23% compared with the year-ago period.

Stripping out the impact of exchange rates and higher gas prices, comparable sales rose 6.2%.

The company said the retail month had one fewer shopping day compared with last year, negatively impacting total sales by about 1.5%.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: AI Data-Center Developer's Earnings Top Views. Why Applied Digital Stock Is Moving Higher. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kit Norton

Applied Digital's fiscal 2026 third-quarter results broadly topped Wall Street estimates after the closing bell on Wednesday, as the company said it sees accelerating demand for AI data-center capacity.

The Dallas, Texas.,-based AI data-center developer announced late Wednesday adjusted earnings of 9 cents per share, well above Wall Street's consensus view of a 16-cent loss, according to FactSet. The company also reported revenue growing 139% to $126.64 million, topping expectations for sales totaling $75.5 million.

Applied Digital shares jumped 2.4% after the closing bell Wednesday. The stock rose 11% to $27.87 during regular trading, as many stocks rallied following news of a cease-fire in the Iran conflict. For comparison, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite gained 2.8% on Wednesday.

Write to Kit Norton at kit.norton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Garda Therapeutics to Acquire Assertio Holdings for $125.1M in Cash
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Assertio Holdings agreed to be acquired by Garda Therapeutics for $125.1 million in cash.

The pharmaceutical company also said it signed and closed an agreement to sell all non-Rolvedon assets to Cosette Pharmaceuticals for an up-front payment of $35 million, plus earnouts related to product milestones.

It said the Cosette transaction is reflected in the total cash consideration for the Garda acquisition, which represents a price of $18 per share.

Garda's price represents a roughly 35% premium to the company's stock price on March 20, and a 62% premium to the company's 60-day unaffected volume-weighted average price as of that day, Assertio said.

In addition to the cash payment, Garda will also issue a non-tradable contingent value right for potential future milestones of the company's Spirx drug.

Garda will acquire shares through a tender offer, Assertio said. Following the tender offer, Garda will acquire all non-tendered shares through a second-step merger at the same price of $18 a share.

The boards of directors of both companies have unanimously agreed to the transaction, which is expected to close in the second quarter.

Shares of Assertio fell slightly to $18.29 in after-hours trading Wednesday. The stock closed down 1% at $18.41, having doubled in value year to date.

Assertio Board Chair Heather Mason said the company evaluated multiple strategic options, including a sale, merger opportunities, or continuing as a standalone opportunity. It engaged more than 35 counterparties, including strategic and financial buyers, she said.

"Following this thorough process — and with the addition under the agreement for an incremental shop period to ensure maximum value — the board has determined that these transactions with Cosette and Garda provide the best outcome for our shareholders," Mason said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Argan Approves $50M Increase to Share-Buyback Program
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Argan approved an increase of its share-buyback program to $200 million.

Alongside the increase from $150 million, the board also approved the buyback program's extension through Jan. 31, 2030.

The construction company also declared a quarterly dividend of 50 cents a share, which will be payable on April 30 to shareholders of record as of April 22.

The dividend is the same amount as the previous two quarters. It represents an annual payout of $2 a share and represents a yield of 0.3% based on Wednesday's closing price of $588.28.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Western Alliance Bancorporation Announces First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release Date, Conference Call and Webcast
data.description:
PHOENIX--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 08, 2026--

Western Alliance Bancorporation announced today that it plans to release its first quarter 2026 financial results after the market closes on Tuesday, April 21, 2026. Kenneth A. Vecchione, President and CEO and Vishal Idnani, Chief Financial Officer, will host a conference call at 12:00 p.m. ET on Wednesday, April 22, 2026 to discuss the Company's performance.

Participants may access the call by dialing 1-888-596-4144 using the access code 9350603 or via live audio webcast using the website link: https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/403697580.

The webcast is also available through the Company's website at www.westernalliancebancorporation.com. Participants should log in at least 15 minutes early to receive instructions. The call will be recorded and made available for replay April 22(nd) after 3:00 p.m. ET until April 29(th) at 11:59 p.m. ET by dialing 1-800-770-2030 using the access code: 9350603.

About Western Alliance Bancorporation

Western Alliance Bancorporation is one of the country's top-performing banking companies. Its primary subsidiary, Western Alliance Bank, Member FDIC, is a leading national bank for business that puts customers first, delivering tailored business banking solutions and consumer products backed by outstanding, personalized service and specific expertise in more than 30 industries and sectors. With $90 billion in assets and offices nationwide, Western Alliance has ranked as a top U.S. bank by American Banker and Bank Director since 2016. In 2025, Western Alliance Bancorporation was #2 for Best CEO, Best CFO and Best Company Board of Directors on Extel's All-America Executive Team Midcap Banks list. For more information on offerings, subsidiaries and affiliates, visit www.westernalliancebank.com or follow Western Alliance Bank on LinkedIn.

data.title: GAME: Revenue surged 63% to $45M, but losses and going concern risks persist
data.description:
Revenue grew 63% to $45M in 2025, driven by agency expansion and digital asset yield, while net loss narrowed to $42.1M. Key acquisitions, a new crypto treasury strategy, and continued share buybacks marked the year, but material weaknesses in internal controls and going concern risks remain.

Original document: GameSquare Holdings, Inc. [GAME] SEC 10-K Annual Report — Apr. 8 2026
